purpose: A closed-world predicate no fact, graph attribute, or rule head ever grounds: with 'busy' registered closed-world and wholly unstated, the single-clause rule flag(x) <-1 busy(x):[0,0] takes the ALL-NODES fallback grounding (the predicate map has no 'busy' entry, so get_rule_node_clause_grounding falls back to every node, interpretation.py:1396-1402) and the CWA branch reads each node's missing label as [0,0] — flag fires [1,1] for John, Mary, AND Justin at t=1, while 'busy' itself leaves no row anywhere; the ordinary (non-closed-world) never-grounded edge rule alongside stays completely silent — empty link frames and an empty edge trace. Contrast closed-world-on, where a stated fact narrows the grounding to the predicate map.
provenance: corpus seed — docs/surface.md fn:add_closed_world_predicate interacts-unknown-predicate (the session-20 board note's ready-made case shape, screened live 2026-07-07, re-screened 2026-07-12: 4 fresh pin processes, digest-stable); interpretation.py:1396-1402, :1757-1775 at the pin
